user_prompt: Describe Bernoulli’s principle through an airplane wing example in depth.

style_bible.visual_style: clean scientific visualization, realistic airplane wing with semi-transparent airflow streamlines
style_bible.camera_rules: smooth, deliberate movements with pauses for explanation; no abrupt motion
style_bible.lighting_rules: neutral daylight, high contrast between wing surface and airflow lines
style_bible.continuity_rules: airplane wing orientation and airflow direction must remain consistent across shots and clips

clips.clip_script: Introduce the airplane wing in flight and visualize airflow paths around it, setting up Bernoulli’s principle.

shots.framing: wide side view of airplane wing against sky
shots.subject_action: airplane wing glides smoothly as airflow streamlines appear
shots.continuity_note: airflow direction established, wing angle remains constant into next shot

shots.subject_action: airflow lines clearly curve over and under the wing
shots.continuity_note: close-up establishes airflow speed contrast for next shot

shots.framing: angled view emphasizing upper wing surface
shots.subject_action: upper airflow accelerates visually compared to below
shots.continuity_note: FINAL FRAME HANDOFF: camera holds on wing with visible faster airflow above, frozen for next clip start

clips.negative_prompt: logos, watermarks, brand marks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit, unreadable tiny text

clips.clip_script: Explain pressure differences caused by airflow speed and show how this creates lift on the wing.

shots.framing: same angled wing view continuing from previous clip
shots.subject_action: pressure color map fades in above and below wing
shots.continuity_note: continues directly from final frame of clip 1 with same wing position

shots.subject_action: low pressure above wing highlighted, higher pressure below emphasized
shots.continuity_note: pressure contrast clearly established for lift explanation

shots.framing: slightly wider view showing wing lifting against airflow
shots.subject_action: upward force arrows appear, indicating lift
shots.continuity_note: FINAL FRAME HANDOFF: wing held steady with lift arrows visible, concluding explanation

clips.negative_prompt: logos, watermarks, brand marks, extra limbs, distorted faces, flicker, glitch, people, human, face, hands, cockpit, unreadable tiny text

full_script_20s: Show how air flows around an airplane wing, then explain Bernoulli’s principle by comparing air speed and pressure above and below the wing, concluding with how this pressure difference creates lift.
